description_long: This is a bulbous, cream-colored ceramic vase with a wide, flared mouth and a rounded body that tapers into a sculpted, irregular base. The surface is adorned with incised geometric patterns and stylized leaf motifs, giving it a hand-crafted, artisanal appearance. A single, thick handle is attached to one side, featuring a roughly circular opening and blending into the body with visible seams. The rim of the vase is uneven and slightly chipped, suggesting age or intentional rustic design. The finish is matte with areas of discoloration and patina, indicating exposure to the elements or long-term use. The overall form is organic and slightly asymmetrical, contributing to its decorative, antique charm.
description: A cream-colored, bulbous ceramic vase with a wide flared mouth, incised geometric and leaf patterns, and a single thick handle. The surface is matte and weathered, with an irregular base and rim.
description_view_0: Front view shows the flared mouth, rounded body, and incised patterns, with the handle not visible.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thick handle attached to the body and more of the geometric carvings.
description_view_2: Rear view focuses on the handle's attachment and the irregular base, with less visible patterning.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the handle from a different angle and the continuation of the carved motifs.
